proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0 after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model including demographics, biomarkers, labs, comorbidities, metastatic sites, and treatments.
analyses.result_summary: `stage_iv` was associated with 1.361 fewer months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.00776; t=-175.51).

iterations.update: Advanced disease burden was a dominant adverse prognostic factor, so subsequent iterations examined other baseline severity markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-point increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each higher ECOG performance status point was associated with 1.171 fewer months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.00570; t=-205.69).

iterations.update: Performance status had a very large independent association, motivating checks of age and frailty-related markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `age_years` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each additional year of age was associated with 0.1758 higher months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.00039; t=453.36).

iterations.update: The age association was positive rather than adverse, so later iterations tested whether age interacted with severity markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each additional percentage point of six-month weight loss was associated with 0.0761 fewer months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.00100; t=-76.05).

iterations.update: Cachexia markers showed strong adverse prognosis; albumin and inflammatory markers were tested next.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL higher albumin was associated with 0.469 higher months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.00770; t=60.99).

iterations.update: Better nutritional/inflammatory status tracked with longer PFS; tumor marker burden was examined next.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `cea_ng_ml` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 ng/mL higher CEA was associated with 0.0050 fewer months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.00024; t=-20.99).

iterations.update: CEA was a significant adverse marker and later became an important regorafenib effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 0 after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation` was associated with 0.354 fewer months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.00807; t=-43.93).

iterations.update: RAS biology appeared prognostic, prompting tests of sidedness and targeted-treatment heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 0 after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: `right_sided_primary` was associated with 0.287 fewer months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.00808; t=-35.48).

iterations.update: Right-sided primary location was independently adverse and was retained for treatment-interaction testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 0 after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e` was associated with 0.355 fewer months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.01890; t=-18.79).

iterations.update: BRAF V600E was adverse and was tested as both a biomarker main effect and treatment effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 U/L higher LDH was associated with 0.00043 fewer months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.00005; t=-8.90).

iterations.update: LDH added a smaller but significant adverse burden signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 mg/L higher CRP was associated with 0.00081 fewer months of `pfs_months`; this was borderline but below the 0.05 threshold (SE 0.00041; t=-1.97).

iterations.update: CRP had only a weak independent effect compared with weight loss, albumin, CEA, and LDH.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_regorafenib` have higher `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_regorafenib` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model without treatment-biomarker interactions.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_regorafenib` was associated with 0.943 higher months of `pfs_months` after adjustment (SE 0.00964; t=97.82).

iterations.update: Regorafenib showed the clearest treatment association, so subsequent iterations focused on effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_cetuximab` have higher `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_cetuximab` after adjustment for other available clinical features.

analyses.method: Multivariable OLS main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_cetuximab` was not associated with higher `pfs_months`; the adjusted coefficient was slightly negative and not significant (SE 0.00841; t=-1.31).

iterations.update: Cetuximab did not show a detectable main effect, so RAS and sidedness interactions were checked separately.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `msi_high` equal to 1 than in patients with `msi_high` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Adjusted OLS interaction model including `treatment_pembrolizumab`, `msi_high`, their product, and baseline covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `msi_high` interaction was small and not significant (interaction 0.057 months; SE 0.04848; t=1.18).

iterations.update: No MSI-high pembrolizumab heterogeneity was detected in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_encorafenib` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 than in patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Adjusted OLS interaction model including `treatment_encorafenib`, `braf_v600e`, their product, and baseline covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_encorafenib` by `braf_v600e` interaction was not significant and had a slightly negative sign (interaction -0.017 months; SE 0.06009; t=-0.28).

iterations.update: BRAF V600E was prognostic but did not predict an encorafenib-associated PFS gain here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `her2_amplified` equal to 1 than in patients with `her2_amplified` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Adjusted OLS interaction model including `treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib`, `her2_amplified`, their product, and baseline covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib` by `her2_amplified` interaction was effectively null (interaction 0.0027 months; SE 0.08334; t=0.03).

iterations.update: HER2 amplification did not identify a trastuzumab/tucatinib PFS signal in these data.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_bevacizumab` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `liver_mets` equal to 1 than in patients with `liver_mets` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Adjusted OLS interaction model including `treatment_bevacizumab`, `liver_mets`, their product, and baseline covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_bevacizumab` by `liver_mets` interaction was near zero and not significant (interaction -0.0039 months; SE 0.02188; t=-0.18).

iterations.update: Bevacizumab did not show liver-metastasis-specific heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_regorafenib` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 than in patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Joint adjusted OLS interaction model with regorafenib interactions for `kras_mutation`, `right_sided_primary`, `braf_v600e`, `nras_mutation`, and centered `cea_ng_ml`.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_regorafenib` by `kras_mutation` interaction was strongly negative (interaction -1.763 months; SE 0.01675; t=-105.23).

iterations.update: KRAS mutation substantially attenuated the regorafenib association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_regorafenib` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 1 than in patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Joint adjusted OLS regorafenib interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_regorafenib` by `right_sided_primary` interaction was strongly negative (interaction -1.475 months; SE 0.01679; t=-87.86).

iterations.update: Right-sided primary location independently attenuated the regorafenib association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_regorafenib` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 than in patients with `braf_v600e` equal to 0.

analyses.method: Joint adjusted OLS regorafenib interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_regorafenib` by `braf_v600e` interaction was strongly negative (interaction -1.760 months; SE 0.03887; t=-45.29).

iterations.update: BRAF V600E was both prognostically adverse and a negative modifier of the regorafenib association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_regorafenib` and `pfs_months` decreases as `cea_ng_ml` increases.

analyses.method: Joint adjusted OLS regorafenib interaction model using centered `cea_ng_ml`.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_regorafenib` by centered `cea_ng_ml` interaction was strongly negative: each 1 ng/mL higher CEA reduced the regorafenib association by 0.0249 months (SE 0.00050; t=-49.61).

iterations.update: High CEA marked weaker regorafenib-associated benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_regorafenib` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `nras_mutation` equal to 1 than in patients with `nras_mutation` equal to 0 after accounting for other regorafenib modifiers.

analyses.method: Joint adjusted OLS regorafenib interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The apparent NRAS interaction from unadjusted screening did not remain significant in the joint model (interaction 0.0749 months; SE 0.04691; t=1.60).

iterations.update: NRAS did not add independent predictive information once KRAS, sidedness, BRAF, and CEA were modeled.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 0, `right_sided_primary` equal to 0, `braf_v600e` equal to 0, and `cea_ng_ml` less than 50, those receiving `treatment_regorafenib` have higher mean `pfs_months` than those not receiving it.

analyses.method: Subgroup Welch-style mean contrast for `treatment_regorafenib` within the favorable biomarker subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: In the favorable subgroup, mean `pfs_months` was 6.945 with regorafenib versus 4.117 without regorafenib, a 2.828-month difference (SE 0.05860; t=48.26).

iterations.update: Regorafenib benefit was concentrated in a biomarker-defined favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `age_years` and `pfs_months` is smaller at higher `ecog_ps` values.

analyses.method: Two-way OLS interaction model with centered `age_years`, centered `ecog_ps`, and their product.
analyses.result_summary: The `age_years` by `ecog_ps` interaction was negative: each ECOG point reduced the age slope by 0.0072 months per year (SE 0.00082; t=-8.81).

iterations.update: Age-related heterogeneity existed but was much smaller than the main effects and regorafenib interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combined adverse association of `kras_mutation` equal to 1 and `right_sided_primary` equal to 1 with `pfs_months` is less negative than the sum of their separate adverse associations.

analyses.method: Two-way OLS interaction model with `kras_mutation`, `right_sided_primary`, and their product.
analyses.result_summary: The `kras_mutation` by `right_sided_primary` interaction was positive (0.452 months; SE 0.04325; t=10.44), indicating subadditivity of their separate adverse associations.

iterations.update: The final pass found modest biomarker-biomarker heterogeneity, but the dominant actionable pattern remained the regorafenib modifier set.
